documents.title: Web API | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document provides an introductory overview of the Spotify Web API, outlining the initial steps for app creation, authorization, and the types of music data and playback controls available to developers.

documents.title: Getting started with Web API
documents.summary: This tutorial provides a step-by-step guide for making a first Spotify Web API call, covering app registration, obtaining an access token via client credentials, and retrieving artist metadata using cURL.

documents.title: Client Credentials Flow | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document explains how to implement the OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow for server-to-server authentication to obtain an access token for the Spotify Web API. It outlines the required request parameters, headers, and the resulting JSON response structure.

documents.title: Authorization Code Flow | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document explains how to implement the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow to obtain user authorization and access tokens for long-running applications using the Spotify Web API.

documents.title: Authorization Code with PKCE Flow
documents.summary: This document explains how to implement the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow with PKCE for secure user authentication in public client applications like mobile or single-page apps.

documents.title: Implicit Grant Flow | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document explains the Spotify Implicit Grant flow, a client-side authorization method that allows applications to obtain access tokens without server-side code. It details the steps for constructing authorization requests, handling user redirects, and processing the resulting access tokens.

documents.title: Refreshing tokens | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: A guide explaining how to exchange a refresh token for a new access token using the Spotify OAuth 2.0 flow.

documents.title: Migrate from Implicit Grant Flow to Authorization Code with PKCE
documents.summary: This guide provides instructions for migrating a Web Application from the deprecated Implicit Grant Flow to the Authorization Code with PKCE flow for the Spotify Web API.

documents.title: Migrate away from Insecure Redirect URIs
documents.summary: This guide provides instructions for migrating Spotify applications from insecure redirect URIs to secure alternatives like HTTPS and loopback addresses.

documents.title: February 2026 Web API Dev Mode Changes - Migration Guide
documents.summary: This migration guide provides instructions for developers to update their Spotify Development Mode applications in response to the 2026 Web API changes, covering endpoint consolidations, field renames, and new app restrictions.

documents.title: Apps | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document provides step-by-step instructions for registering and configuring an application on the Spotify Developer Dashboard to obtain essential authentication credentials like the Client ID and Client Secret.

documents.title: Authorization | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document explains the Spotify implementation of the OAuth 2.0 authorization framework, detailing how applications obtain permissions and which specific authorization flows to use based on application architecture.

documents.title: Access Token | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document explains the purpose and usage of access tokens for API authorization, including the required header format and token expiration details.

documents.title: API calls | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document outlines the fundamental conventions of the Spotify Web API, covering request methods, response status codes, error handling formats, and data standards like timestamps and pagination.

documents.title: Scopes | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document defines and describes the various authorization scopes required to access specific Spotify Web API endpoints and manage user permissions.

documents.title: Spotify URIs and IDs | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document defines the various resource identifiers and URL formats used for identifying artists, albums, tracks, and users within the Spotify Web API.

documents.title: Playlists | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document explains how to manage and interact with Spotify playlists through the Web API, covering visibility statuses, required authorization scopes, and the technical handling of both Spotify-hosted and local tracks.

documents.title: Rate Limits | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document explains the Spotify Web API rate limiting mechanism and provides strategies for developers to manage request volume and handle 429 error responses.

documents.title: Quota modes | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document outlines the differences between Spotify's development and extended quota modes, detailing user limitations, allowlisting procedures, and the application process for increasing app access.

documents.title: Track Relinking | Spotify for Developers
documents.summary: This document explains the track relinking mechanism in the Spotify Web API, detailing how the market parameter can be used to find playable alternatives for tracks unavailable in certain regions.

documents.title: Display your Spotify profile data in a web app
documents.summary: This guide provides step-by-step instructions for building a client-side application that authenticates users via the Spotify Web API using the PKCE authorization flow. It demonstrates how to retrieve and display a user's profile information using TypeScript or JavaScript.
